description: AWS Labs open-source AI-DLC (AI-Driven Development Life Cycle) — structured phase-gated workflow for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, Q Developer)
key_patterns: Phase-gated workflow: INCEPTION → CONSTRUCTION → OPERATIONS with explicit human approval gates
Workspace detection (brownfield vs greenfield) before ANY code generation
539-line core-workflow.md replaces CLAUDE.md as a process playbook, not style guide
28 rule detail files loaded on demand per stage
Extensions system with opt-in prompts (security, testing, resiliency) — rules only loaded if user opts in
Audit trail: every interaction logged to aidlc-docs/audit.md with raw user input
Adaptive depth: simple requests get minimal ceremony, complex get comprehensive
Session continuity: resumption across sessions via aidlc-state.md
Plan-level checkbox enforcement — every step tracked in same interaction
Question file format: structured multiple-choice markdown files, not chat messages
steal_for_nebula: 1) Installed AI-DLC rules to .aidlc/ — any coding agent now follows structured workflow
3) Upgraded CLAUDE.md from 38-line style guide to 65-line process playbook + key facts
4) audit.md + aidlc-state.md initialized for full interaction traceability
risk: AWS pattern assumes software dev with Claude Code — Nebula adaptations needed for multi-agent business pipeline context
